name: Old-California Empanaditas
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter, cream cheese, and salt together in a mixer until completely smooth and blended.
2. Work in flour to form a smooth dough. For best results, work with your fingertips, a fork, or rubber spatula rather than using a mixer for this step.
3. Flatten the dough onto a sheet of foil to create an 8 x 6 inch rectangle.
4. Chill the dough overnight (several days before use is acceptable).
5. Remove from the refrigerator 8 to 10 minutes before rolling out.
6. Divide the dough into manageable pieces to facilitate rolling and ensure even coldness throughout.
7. Store any unrolled dough in the refrigerator until ready for use.
8. Roll the dough on a floured pastry cloth with a floured rolling pin, or between two sheets of waxed paper. For easier handling, flour both the cloth and pin well before rolling, then shake off excess flour. When using waxed paper, loosen it slightly on both sides during rolling to prevent sticking.
9. Shape according to specific recipes and chill before baking.
10. Brush only the tops of all pastries with an egg yolk beaten with cream.
11. Bake in a moderate oven (350 degrees F) unless otherwise specified.
